documents.text: Metformin is a first-line oral medication for type 2 diabetes. It works by reducing hepatic glucose production and improving insulin sensitivity. Common side effects include gastrointestinal upset. It is generally weight-neutral.
Statins lower LDL cholesterol by inhibiting HMG-CoA reductase. They reduce the risk of cardiovascular events in high-risk patients. Muscle pain is a known but uncommon adverse effect.

Type 2 diabetes is characterized by insulin resistance and relative insulin deficiency. Lifestyle modification, including diet and exercise, is a cornerstone of management. Untreated hyperglycemia can lead to microvascular complications.
Aspirin is an antiplatelet agent used in secondary prevention of myocardial infarction. It irreversibly inhibits cyclooxygenase. Bleeding risk must be weighed against cardiovascular benefit.

questions.question: What is the first-line medication for type 2 diabetes?
questions.ideal_answer: Metformin is the first-line oral medication for type 2 diabetes [1].

questions.question: How do statins lower cholesterol?
questions.ideal_answer: Statins inhibit HMG-CoA reductase, which lowers LDL cholesterol [1].

questions.question: What lifestyle changes help manage type 2 diabetes?
questions.ideal_answer: Diet and exercise are cornerstones of managing type 2 diabetes [1].

questions.question: Why is aspirin used after a heart attack?
questions.ideal_answer: Aspirin is an antiplatelet agent used in secondary prevention of myocardial infarction [1].
